Subscribe by RSS

A ping in your reader whenever a new call lands on the desk. Pick a slice if you want a focused feed.

Categories
feed.xml
Open feed
How to subscribe (RSS reader or email)
  1. Optionally tick one or more Categories, pick a Region, and/or pick a Timeline to narrow the feed. If you tick multiple categories, you'll get one feed URL per category, which you can subscribe to separately.
  2. Copy the feed URL above (or click Open feed to preview it).
  3. Paste the URL into either:
    • An RSS reader — Feedly, NetNewsWire, Inoreader, Reeder, Thunderbird. New entries appear in the reader as they're added.
    • A free RSS-to-email service for an inbox digest, no account needed here:
      • Kill the Newsletter — dead simple, no signup; generates a unique address that turns each new feed item into a plain email.
      • Blogtrottr — pick realtime, daily or weekly digest.
      • Feedrabbit — similar digest controls, free up to a few feeds.
  4. Deadline-filtered feeds (Next 30 / 90 days) only include grants with a fixed deadline in that window. Rolling/undated grants are excluded from those.
  5. "Added in last 30 days" includes everything added recently regardless of deadline (great for a "what's new" feed and the closest match to a newsletter when paired with a weekly email digest).

Add deadlines to your calendar

A live calendar of upcoming deadlines. Stays in sync as new calls land or details change.

Categories
calendar.ics
Download once
How to subscribe
  1. Optionally tick one or more Categories and/or pick a Region to narrow the calendar. If you tick multiple categories, you'll get one .ics URL per category, which you can subscribe to separately.
  2. Copy the calendar URL.
  3. Google Calendar: "Other calendars" → "+" → "From URL", paste, Add.
  4. Apple Calendar: "File" → "New Calendar Subscription", paste, OK.
  5. Outlook: "Add calendar" → "Subscribe from web", paste.
  6. Your calendar refreshes every few hours, so new grants and changes flow in automatically.

Share the desk

Pass it along. Short link or QR, whichever fits.

Suggest a grant Buy me a coffee

Grants, Fellowships and Residencies in AI, Arts, Film, Research and Tech The Grant Desk

-- active grants on the desk right now. The Grant Desk is a free tool. No account, paywall or subscription required. The site consists of a hand-picked selection of paid open calls, fellowships and residencies for artists, journalists, writers, researchers and technologists working in the realm of digital media, AI, and technology and society, with a few notable unpaid open calls and festival submissions included as clearly flagged exceptions. Star a card to save it to a shortlist you can share as a link or download as an .ics calendar, and use the "Copy info" button on any card to copy the full grant details to your clipboard. The desk also remembers when you last visited (saved in your browser's localStorage only, not on any server) so the "Show only new" filter highlights what has been added since.

Subscribe via RSS or add deadlines to your calendar so you do not miss a thing.

WATCHLIST

Watch for next round

Funders whose current cycle is closed but whose next call is expected. Not counted in the active grants tally. Entries are auto-removed once their expected window has passed; check the funder page directly for confirmed dates.

  • J. Anthony Lukas Work-in-Progress Awards 2027 (Columbia Journalism School and Nieman Foundation) . Submissions reopen in fall 2026; the last cycle closed 4 December 2025. Two annual awards of USD 25,000 each to help complete a book-length work of nonfiction on a topic of American political or social concern (science, tech and media accountability books have won before). Applicants normally need a US publishing contract; check the fall announcement for entry requirements and any submission fee.
  • Allianz Foundation Grants Programme (Europe and the Mediterranean) . The 2025 round closed 10 February 2025 and no 2026 call has been announced; watch for the next cycle, which historically opens around January-February. Grants of EUR 80,000 to 200,000 over up to 24 months for organizations (not individuals) in Europe and the Mediterranean working in Arts and Culture, European Civil Society, or Climate and Environment. The foundation covers up to 75 percent of project costs; at least 25 percent must come from other sources. No application fee.
  • European AI & Society Fund (Europe) . Not currently accepting funding requests; new calls are announced via the fund's newsletter. Pooled fund (Bosch, Mott, Ford, Luminate, Mozilla, Oak, Open Society, Mercator and others) that finances civil society organizations shaping AI in the public interest, through the Build initiative (sustained flexible funding for tech and social justice groups across Europe) and the Breakthrough initiative (EUR 10 million against harmful AI impacts). Organizations only. No application fee.
  • Culture Helps Solidarity (European Cultural Foundation, Creative Europe) . First rounds closed in spring 2026 (Collaboration Grants 31 March, Project Grants 16 April, Individual Care Grants 18 May); more than 20 further rounds follow through March 2028. Over EUR 1.4 million total for cultural organizations, artists and community partners supporting Ukrainian cultural cooperation and displaced people, via Individual Care Grants, Project Grants and Collaboration Grants between Ukrainian and European organizations. No application fee.
  • Bloomberg New Contemporaries 2027 (UK) . The 2026 open call closed 30 March 2026; the next is expected February-March 2027. Flagship UK platform for emerging and early-career artists aged 21+ based in England, Northern Ireland, Scotland or Wales: selected artists join a year-long Artist Development Programme and a touring group exhibition at major venues. Submit up to three works in any medium. Note: GBP 25 non-refundable application fee.
  • Bloomberg Data Science Ph.D. Fellowship 2027-28 . The 2026-27 round closed 14 December 2025; the next call is expected autumn 2026 with a mid-December deadline. Covers 100 percent of tuition plus a USD 45,000 stipend for living, conference and research expenses, with a required 14-week paid summer internship at Bloomberg each fellowship year. For full-time doctoral students in data science, AI and computer science (Ph.D. expected by end of 2029 for the current cycle); apply with CV, two-page research proposal and advisor reference. No visa sponsorship.
  • Bibliothek Andreas Zuest Residency 2027 (Oberegg, Switzerland) . Next call expected October 2026 (for summer 2027 residencies), announced via the library's Instagram and newsletter. Six-week fully funded residency at the Alpenhof: free lodging (private bedroom), free meals on-site, travel costs covered, and a CHF 750/month subsistence allowance on request. Open to international and domestic cultural practitioners across all disciplines (visual arts, literature, new media, music, performance, design, architecture, film, photography, research); duos and collectives up to five people welcome.
  • Guggenheim Fellowship 2027 . The 2026 United States and Canada competition is closed; the 2027 competition opens for applications in late summer 2026 (applications typically close mid September). Variable cash grant, usually tens of thousands of US dollars, for 6 to 12 months of supported work; around 175 to 200 fellows selected annually across more than 50 fields in the arts, humanities, natural sciences and social sciences. Open to US and Canadian citizens and permanent residents; no application fee.
  • Ford Foundation JustFilms 2027 . Next call expected spring 2027 (the 2026 open call closed around 10 April). Grants typically USD 25,000-100,000, no application fee. Annual four-week open call for artist-driven, feature-length (50+ min) documentaries intersecting with social justice; open to filmmakers globally, with focus on regions where Ford has offices.
  • EMAP Residencies 2027 . Next call expected autumn 2026. EUR 4,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 collaborator stipend + EUR 4,000 production budget + accommodation + travel. Two-month production residency at one of thirteen European host organisations for artists working in digital, media, robotic, AI or bio art.
  • Onassis AiR . Next call expected early 2027. Stipend, accommodation, travel and working space for a 10-week residency in Athens; about thirty fellows per cohort across visual arts, performance, sound, art and advanced tech, curatorial and writing.
  • Tarbell Fellowship 2027 (AI Journalism) . Next call expected late 2026. USD 60,000-80,000 (regular fellows) or USD 90,000-110,000 (senior fellows) for a 12-month placement at major US/UK newsrooms building an AI-coverage beat.
  • GovAI Summer Fellowship 2027 . Next call expected late 2026. Three-month AI-governance fellowship with mentors at the Centre for the Governance of AI; tracks include Research (London), Applied (GBP 12,000) and a DC-based track (USD 21,000 plus London travel support).
  • Tech Policy Press Fellowship 2027 . Next call expected September or October 2026. 12-month part-time fellowship for independent contributors writing on tech and democracy. USD 10,000 stipend for monthly reporting and analysis contributions.
  • openDemocracy x Tech Policy Press Tech Reporting Fellowship 2027 . Next call expected late 2026. UK-embedded tech reporting fellowship for journalists (including researchers, lawyers and technologists transitioning into journalism), double-published by openDemocracy and Tech Policy Press. GBP 30,000 per annum over 12 months.
  • Knight Science Journalism Fellowship 2027-28 (MIT) . Next call expected around 9 January 2027. Nine-month residential fellowship at MIT for journalists with 3+ consecutive years covering science, health, tech or environmental issues. USD 85,000 stipend plus travel, housing and basic health insurance.
  • Reuters Institute Journalist Fellowship 2027-28 (Oxford) . Next call expected January 2027 (for cohorts starting October 2027, January 2028, April 2028). Mid-career residential fellowship at Oxford to study journalism, AI and digital transformation in depth. Fully funded with a stipend for living and travel costs.
  • Nieman Fellowship 2027-28 (Harvard) . Next call opens first week of October 2026; deadlines 1 December 2026 (international) and 31 January 2027 (US). Academic-year fellowship at Harvard with stipend, tuition, housing and health insurance for working journalists pursuing a self-directed project, often in tech/digital culture directions.
  • Andrew Carnegie Fellows 2027 . Nominations expected to open September 2026 and close around early November 2026. USD 200,000 over 1-2 years for US-based scholars, journalists and public intellectuals on a book-length project, usually framed around US political polarisation and cohesion. Institutional nomination required.
  • NEH Public Scholars Program 2027 . Next call expected April 2027. USD 30,000 to USD 60,000 per grant for non-fiction book projects aimed at general audiences. Open to US citizens and permanent residents; independent writers and journalists explicitly encouraged.
  • Heinrich Boell Transatlantic Media Fellowship 2027 . Next call expected around March 2027. Transatlantic reporting stipend for journalists in any medium: USD 4,000 (on-the-ground with travel) or USD 2,000 (digital/virtual fellowship). For US/Canada-based journalists writing on the EU, or German journalists writing on the US/Canada. Distinct from the Foundation's Global Majority AI Fellowship that is already on the desk.
  • Saari Residence 2028 (Kone Foundation, Finland) . Next call expected autumn 2026 (for 2028 residencies). EUR 2,700-3,800/month (experience-tiered) plus a two-month residency in Mynamaki for international writers, translators, composers, curators and critics. The 2028 cycle has reduced capacity due to renovation.
  • RSL Giles St Aubyn Awards for Non-Fiction 2027 . Next call expected September 2026. GBP 10,000 / 5,000 / 2,500 across three tiered awards for UK or Ireland-based first-time nonfiction writers with a contract for their first commissioned book.
  • Robert B. Silvers Foundation Grants for Work in Progress 2027 . Next call expected around March 2027. Up to USD 10,000 for long-form critics and essayists worldwide working on literary or arts criticism, intellectual essay, political analysis or social reportage. Must have a publishing agreement.
  • Berton House Writers' Residency 2027 (Writers' Trust of Canada) . Next call expected around March 2027. Two months housing, travel covered and CAD 4,000 stipend at Pierre Berton's childhood home in Dawson City, Yukon for writers in poetry, fiction, creative nonfiction, playwriting or children's literature. Verify international eligibility (some years have been Canadian-only).
  • PEN Translates 2027 (English PEN) . Next round expected November 2026 or May 2027 (biannual). Funds UK publishers (and global publishers via PEN Translates x SALT) for up to 75% of literary translation costs into English, 100% for small publishers; the translator is paid by the publisher. Distinct from the PEN America PEN/Heim Translation Fund already on the desk.
  • Tejumola Olaniyan Creative Writers Fellowship 2027 (The Africa Institute, Sharjah) . Next call expected early 2027. AED 50,000 (approximately USD 13,600) residency fellowship at The Africa Institute in Sharjah for writers from Africa and its diaspora engaging with Africa and African diaspora themes. Any genre.
  • Mophradat Writing Sabbaticals 2026-27 . Next round expected late 2026 (biannual). Sabbatical stipend (amount varies) plus peer editor support for published Arab fiction writers based in the Arab world finalising a manuscript. Priority for those writing in Arabic.
  • Goethe-Institut Documenta Fellowship 2027 . Next call expected around January 2027. EUR 2,000/month for six months plus visa, housing and travel support for a research fellowship at the documenta archive in Kassel; open to non-German-resident writers, scholars and curators researching contemporary art and documenta. German C1 ideally required.
  • Commonwealth Short Story Prize 2027 . Opens 1 September 2026; deadline typically early November 2026. GBP 5,000 to overall winner; GBP 2,500 to regional winners. International short story prize for Commonwealth citizens aged 18+; translations welcome. Free entry.
  • Akademie Schloss Solitude TEXTUAL Fellowship 2028-30 . Next call expected autumn 2027 (triennial cycle, for 2028-30 fellowships). EUR 1,300+/month stipend plus housing and travel for 6 or 9 months in Stuttgart; for early-to-mid-career international writers in literature, translation and dramatic writing (under the "TEXTUAL" sphere). Eligibility window: 2-10 years of independent practice after training.
  • Stage IX Residency (NXT Museum, Amsterdam) . Next call expected late 2026 or early 2027. Practice-based 8-10 month residency at NXT Museum (the Netherlands' first museum dedicated to art-tech-science-sound). Emerging track: EUR 18,000 artist fee plus EUR 5,000 R&D; Established track: EUR 26,000 plus EUR 15,000. Practice areas include spatial sound, VR/XR/AR, gamified/interactive media, kinetic and light-based installation, scenography and immersive theatre.
  • Mesh Festival 2026/27 (HEK Basel) . Next call expected Q4 2026 for the 2027 edition. Co-organised by HEK Basel, HGK Basel FHNW and iart ag - a festival presenting experimental art-tech projects in Basel and Muenchenstein. Selected projects are presented at the October festival; verify the fee/commission structure in the next round announcement.
  • ZKM Rauschenberg Residencies 2027/28 (Karlsruhe) . Next call expected early 2027. Three-month residency at ZKM Karlsruhe (with the Robert Rauschenberg Foundation) for artists bridging performative art and creative technology. EUR 4,000 scholarship plus EUR 1,000 travel, accommodation and up to EUR 2,500 materials budget.
  • Harvestworks 2027 Artists-in-Residence (NYC) . Next call expected late 2026. Year-long NYC residency at the Harvestworks T.E.A.M. Lab for US-based artists making interactive, audio/video, AR/VR or ML-based work, with an assigned engineer. The 2027 stipend is not yet confirmed - verify before applying.
  • FACT Digital Artist Residencies 2027 (Liverpool) . Next call expected early 2027. GBP 4,500 per resident plus Unreal Engine training and curatorial/creative mentoring. 18-week paid residency for early-career artists living in North West England or North Wales, in partnership with Lucid Games, Homotopia and DaDa.
  • Michigan Central x Newlab Fellows-in-Residence 2027 (Detroit) . Next call expected early 2027. USD 30,000 unrestricted plus fabrication facilities, research access and mentorship for a 12-week Detroit-based residency at the intersection of art, technology and society. Practice areas include data visualisation, wearable tech, accessibility, sustainability and art + health tech.
  • ZER01NE Creator Open Call 2027 (Hyundai, Seoul) . Next call expected through 2026 for the 2027 cycle. Hyundai Motor Group's annual open call for art/tech creators; selected projects receive financial support, mentorship and a showcase at ZER01NE Day in Seoul each October. Themes: AI, climate tech, robotics and future mobility.
  • Creative Capital Award 2028 (US) . Next call expected around April 2027. Up to USD 50,000 unrestricted project grant (Creative Capital Award) plus USD 10,000 State of the Art Prize. Major US unrestricted artist grant with a dedicated "Technology" discipline plus multidisciplinary and socially engaged forms; open to US-based artists in all 50 states and territories.
  • Somerset House Studios x UNESCO AI Ethics Residency . Next round expected around early 2027 (the last call closed 15 January 2026). GBP 7,500 artist fee plus GBP 7,500 production budget for a London residency on the ethics of AI; the previous round was open internationally. Somerset House's other programmes (Creative Technologies Fellowship, n-Space) recur on the same page.
  • Disruption Network Institute Fellowships, Round 3 (Berlin) . Next round expected around September 2026. Paid, largely remote investigative fellowships on AI in warfare, surveillance of migrants, and technological control, publishing through the Disruption Network Lab.
  • Berkman Klein Center Fellowships 2027-28 (Harvard) . Next call expected autumn 2026 (last deadline was 5 December). Stipends up to USD 75,000 per year for fellows working on AI and society, privacy, and digital rights; open internationally, practitioners and artists included.
  • Chaos Communication Congress: Art & Installations (Hamburg) . Call for participation historically opens late September with a late-October deadline for the December Congress. Unpaid exhibition slot, but a flagship venue for counter-surveillance and critical tech art.
  • HollyShorts Film Festival 2027 (Los Angeles) . Submissions expected to open around late August 2026 (earlybird historically closes end of January). Oscar-qualifying shorts festival; fees typically USD 45-90 by tier; international.
  • IndieCade 2027 (Los Angeles) . Submissions expected to open around February-March 2027 (the 2026 cycle closed 15 June). International festival of independent games and interactive media, including experimental and installation-based work; fees approx. USD 80-135.
  • Feminist Center for Creative Work: Artist in Residence (LA) . Next call expected late 2026 (last closed 30 November 2025). Project residencies supporting feminist creative practice; prioritizes artists who can be consistently in Los Angeles; free to apply.
  • City of LA COLA-IMAP Fellowship 2027-28 (LA residents only) . FY2027-28 cycle expected to open late September 2026 with an end-of-October deadline. Historically USD 10,000 for mid-career City of Los Angeles artists to premiere a new solo work; LA residents only.
  • Digital Arts Studios Belfast: Home Residency . Next cycle (December 2026 to March 2027) call expected around autumn 2026. Four-month digital art residency with studio access and support; aimed at artists based in Ireland or the UK; free to apply.
  • Cove Park Awarded Residencies (Scotland) . Next general open call expected autumn 2026, covering fully funded residencies (fees, travel, accommodation) April 2027 to December 2028; eligibility strands vary by funder, some international.
  • Thunderbird Drone Festival (Oklahoma State University-Tulsa) . 2026 (6th) edition not yet announced; the previous edition's entries deadline was 25 August with the festival in October. Drone film and photo competition with awards. Use the official OSU page only; the festival's old .com domain appears hijacked.
  • Drone Film Festival Poland (Wroclaw) . The 2026 call typically opens in autumn for the December festival. Crystal Drone awards across six categories including AI and FPV; international.
  • Surveillance Studies Network Arts Prize . Biennial arts prize for work engaging with surveillance; next edition expected 2027-28. The affiliated journal Surveillance & Society also accepts artist contributions on a rolling basis via its arts editor.
  • Bertha Artivism Awards 2027 . Applications open August 2026 (closing date not yet published; cohort starts January 2027). Up to USD 20,000 for a one-year activist-art project made with a social justice organization, campaign, or movement; individuals and collectives worldwide; free to apply.
  • FILE 2027: Electronic Language International Festival (Sao Paulo) . Next call expected around December 2026 to February 2027 (the 2026 call closed 19 February). Latin America's leading electronic/media art festival, free to enter, international, with an explicit AI-art axis.
  • CIFO x Ars Electronica Awards 2027 (Latin American artists only) . Next call expected mid to late 2026 (2026 winners were announced December 2025). USD 45,000 total across awardees plus exhibition at Ars Electronica Linz; Latin American media artists only.
  • Ambulante Gira de Documentales 2027 (Mexico) . The 2027 call should open around August 2026 with a September deadline (the 2026 call closed 22 September 2025). Free entry; Mexican documentaries prioritized, and the Pulsos section accepts non-Mexican productions shot in or about Mexico.
  • FICG 42: Guadalajara International Film Festival . Submissions expected September to December 2026 for the April 2027 festival. One of Latin America's most important festivals; primarily Ibero-American competition focus, check category eligibility.
  • FICCI 66: Cartagena International Film Festival . The FICCI 66 call should open in the second half of 2026 with a deadline around December (FICCI 65 closed 8 December 2025). Latin America's oldest film festival; the previous cycle offered a USD 100,000 prize pool.
  • Radio Tsonami / Tsonami Sound Art Festival (Valparaiso) . The Radio Tsonami broadcast open call (international, radio and sound works) should recur around October 2026; residency calls for the XX-anniversary festival (November 2027) closed June 2026.
  • Pivo Research Residency 2027 (Sao Paulo) . Next call expected around March 2027. Free, internationally open research residency in the Copan building with curatorial and institutional support; Pivo Salvador's separate Brazil-prioritized call recurs around October-November.
  • Chinati Foundation Artist in Residence (Marfa) . Next open call: January 2027 for the 2028-2029 cohorts. Two-month residency with an apartment at the museum, a downtown studio, and USD 4,000 for honorarium, travel, and materials; open internationally, all media.
  • Frameline Completion Fund . Next cycle expected around late September 2026 (the 2025 deadline was 26 September). Grants up to USD 5,000 to complete LGBTQ+ films across documentary, narrative, experimental, animated, and episodic work; explicitly welcomes international applicants; requires a full working cut.
  • BFI Flare 2027: London LGBTQIA+ Film Festival (40th edition) . The 2027 call is expected to open in autumn 2026 with a deadline around early December (the 2026 call closed 5 December 2025). Europe's flagship queer film festival; shorts, mid-length, and features; fees historically low or none.
  • Roze Filmdagen 2027: 30th Amsterdam LGBTQ+ Film Festival . Expect a deadline around 1 December 2026 (the 29th edition closed 1 December 2025; the FilmFreeway page for the 30th already exists). Fiction and documentary competitions with prizes; historically free to submit; festival 10-21 March 2027.
  • Fire Island Artist Residency 2027 . The 2027 call is expected to open early 2027 with a deadline around 1 April. Summer residency exclusively for LGBTQ+ visual artists: housing plus an intensive visiting-critic program; historically open to non-US applicants; modest application fee (~USD 25).
  • Zinegoak 2027: Bilbao LGTBIQA+ Film and Performing Arts Festival . The new call is expected to open around mid-October 2026. International submissions; screening and awards at the Basque Country's queer film and performing arts festival.
  • Berlinale 2027 film submissions . The submission portal for the 77th Berlin International Film Festival (10-21 February 2027) opens in September 2026; deadlines 21 October 2026 (features, 60 min+) and 4 November 2026 (shorts, up to 30 min). Open internationally; entry fee applies (2027 amounts not yet published; recent editions charged roughly EUR 85-190 for features and EUR 30-85 for shorts).
  • DAAD Artists-in-Berlin Program 2028 (Berliner Kuenstlerprogramm) . Applications historically open mid-October to November with a deadline around 15 December; expect the call for 2028 residencies in late 2026. Fully funded ~12-month Berlin residency: monthly grant covering living costs plus apartment/workspace support and travel. Open application ONLY in film, literature, and music and sound (visual arts is nomination-only); open to artists not resident in Germany; no fee.
  • A MAZE. / Berlin 2027 (16th International Games and Playful Media Festival) . Award submissions for the 2026 edition opened in autumn with early bird until the end of the year; expect the 2027 call on a similar schedule. Festival exhibition plus award categories (Most Amazing, Human Human Machine, Digital Moment) for games, XR, alt-controllers, interactive installations and AI works; international; submission fee around EUR 50 (EUR 35 early bird, pay-what-you-want supporter option).
  • Forecast mentorship program (Skills e.V., Radialsystem Berlin) . The 2026 call closed 16 February; the next call is expected between December 2026 and February 2027. Funded mentorship with an international mentor plus festival presentation at Radialsystem Berlin; open internationally across disciplines including sound, digital culture and transmedia practices.
  • Harun Farocki Institut Residency 2027 (Berlin) . Annual call; the 2026 round was published in early February with a spring deadline, so expect the 2027 call around January-February 2027. Three-month funded Berlin residency for film and media artists working in essayistic, documentary and research-based practices; open internationally.
  • Schloss Wiepersdorf fellowships 2028 (Brandenburg) . The 2027 call closed 15 April 2026; the next application window is expected 15 February to 15 April 2027. Residential fellowships near Berlin: EUR 1,200 per month plus room and board plus EUR 480 materials allowance; open internationally across literature, visual arts, composition and cultural research.
  • HTW Berlin IUNO Artistic Fellowship (AI + XR) . The 2026 deadline passed 31 March and required being Berlin-based; a 2027 round is not yet confirmed, so treat this as speculative. EU-funded fellowship at HTW Berlin for artistic work with AI and extended reality; extremely on-profile if a new round opens and if Berlin-based at the time.
ARCHIVE

Filed Away (for 1 year)

Frequently asked questions

What kinds of grants are listed on The Grant Desk?

Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies for artists, researchers and technologists. Categories cover AI and AI safety, digital and mixed-media arts, film and video, research and journalism, tech and infrastructure, and cross-disciplinary or social-impact work. Almost every entry is funded; pure "exposure only" calls and pay-to-play submission fees are generally filtered out, though a few notable unpaid open calls and festival submissions are included as clearly flagged exceptions.

How often is the list updated?

A chronically online human finds candidates by hand from newsletters, Slack and Discord channels, Bluesky, RSS feeds and grant-maker pages, then curates the strong fits. Updates happen periodically rather than on a fixed schedule. Closed calls are auto-filed away after their deadline, so the visible list stays current.

Why are some times of year quieter on the desk?

Funding has seasons. Most big annual cycles close in late autumn or in March and April, with the next wave of restocking typically landing in autumn for January cycles. Mid-spring to early summer tends to be a "dead zone" for new openings, so if the desk looks quiet then, that is usually the funding calendar rather than the desk going stale.

Can I subscribe by RSS or add deadlines to my calendar?

Yes. There is an RSS feed and an ICS calendar feed for every region and category combination. Subscribe in your reader (Feedly, NetNewsWire, etc.) or add the calendar to Google Calendar, Apple Calendar or Outlook so deadlines reach you wherever you already read.

Who runs The Grant Desk, and how can I suggest a grant?

The Grant Desk is maintained by Artificial Nouveau Studio. To suggest a grant, use the "Suggest a grant" form in the top strip; if it is paid and a strong fit, it lands on the desk.

Got a grant to submit?

Send a paid open call my way and I will add it to the desk.

Suggest a grant

Maintained by hand. Sorted automatically by deadline.

Buy me a coffee

(especially if one of these grants comes through)
